_comment: Autotest config for ant-transitive-control-20260525-170044 — Mode-3 path-scan probe. The bolt-4 SCM scanner does NOT invoke Ivy resolution. It path-scans lib/*.jar. 7 JARs are pre-committed reflecting the post-resolution state of three transitive-control mechanisms: plain transitive identity (guava), transitive=false (commons-beanutils), and <exclude> (httpclient). The regression signals are the ABSENT JARs: commons-logging-*.jar and commons-collections-*.jar must NOT appear in the scan output.
_mode_note: Mode-1 Ivy resolution is not supported by bolt-4 SCM scanner. For Mode-1 testing, use pipeline-mode `mend ua` against the same repo.
_warnings_follow_up: nin operator on direct_dependencies_names_list: supported per extending-validations.md (operator 'nin' = none of actual in expected). Used here to assert commons-logging-*.jar and commons-collections-*.jar are absent from Mend's output.

The nin check uses prefix substrings 'commons-logging' and 'commons-collections' which will match any version of these JARs. The nin operator checks that none of the actual dep names are IN the expected list — so listing the full JAR filename (as Mend emits it) is required. Adjust to actual emitted names if Mend uses a different form.
If Mend emits commons-logging or commons-collections JARs sourced from a host-level Maven cache (not from lib/), the nin assertion will fail — this is the intended regression behavior.
description: Autotest configuration file for dependency tree detection validation
